## How This File Works

This file lets you configure project customization offline.
Fill in the values below, check the boxes for options you want,
then apply your choices:

1. Edit the **Value** column in the Project Identity table below.
2. Check the boxes (`[x]`) for options you want to enable.
3. Preview: `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run`
4. Apply: `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md`

> **Tip:** In VS Code, you can click checkboxes directly in the Markdown
> preview to toggle them. The changes will persist to the file.
> Alternatively, edit the raw Markdown: change `[ ]` to `[x]` (or vice versa).

> **Tip:** For a fully non-interactive approach without editing this file,
> use `python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive --project-name NAME
> --author NAME --github-user NAME` with all values on the command line.

---

## Project Identity

Fill in **your** project values. Leave a field empty to keep the template default.

| Setting | Value | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Project name** | `simple-python-boilerplate` | Lowercase, hyphens OK (e.g. `my-cool-app`). Used as repo slug and PyPI name. |
| **Package name** | `simple_python_boilerplate` | Python import name, underscored. Auto-derived from project name if left as default. |
| **Author** | `Joseph` | Author / maintainer name for pyproject.toml. |
| **GitHub user** | `JoJo275` | GitHub username or organization. |
| **Description** | `Simple Python boilerplate using src/ layout` | One-line project description. |
| **CLI prefix** | `spb` | CLI command prefix for entry points (e.g. `spb-version`). Default: initials of project name. |

---

## License

A license defines how others may use, modify, and distribute your code.
Without one, your project is "all rights reserved" by default вЂ” no one
can legally reuse it. Open-source licenses (MIT, Apache, BSD, etc.) grant
explicit permissions and are expected by package registries like PyPI.

Pick **one** license by changing the checkbox to `[x]`:

- [x] **Apache License 2.0** (`apache-2.0`)
- [ ] **MIT License** (`mit`)
- [ ] **BSD 3-Clause License** (`bsd-3-clause`)

---

## Repository Visibility

- [ ] **Private repository** вЂ” Strip open-source community files not needed
  for private repos (CODE_OF_CONDUCT, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, scorecard
  workflow, etc.).

> **Note:** Private repositories on GitHub have a limited amount of Actions
> minutes per month (e.g. 2,000 min/month on the Free plan). CI/CD workflows
> can consume these quickly вЂ” especially matrix builds, container builds, and
> nightly scheduled jobs. Consider disabling non-essential workflows or moving
> heavy jobs to self-hosted runners to stay within your quota.

Files that would be removed:

- **Open-source community files (not needed for private repos)**
  - `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`
  - `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  - `SECURITY.md`
  - `pgp-key.asc`
- **Public-repo workflows (Scorecard, welcome bot, stale issues)**
  - `.github/workflows/scorecard.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/welcome.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/stale.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/sbom.yml`
- **GitHub label management (labels/, apply scripts)**
  - `labels/`
  - `scripts/apply_labels.py`
  - `scripts/apply-labels.sh`

---

## Optional Directories to Strip

Select directories and files you do **not** need.
You can recover anything from git history if needed.

### Bulk Actions

> Use these to apply actions to all items at once without
> checking individual boxes below.

- [ ] **Select all items below** (`select-all`) вЂ” Mark all items for deletion (directory + files)
- [ ] **Delete only files in all items** (`select-all-files-only`) вЂ” Delete files in all items but keep empty directory structures

> Or bypass individual selection entirely:

- [ ] **Delete ALL directories regardless of selection** (`delete-all-dirs`) вЂ” Delete every optional directory and file listed below
- [ ] **Delete ALL files only regardless of selection** (`delete-all-files`) вЂ” Delete files in every optional directory but keep directory structures

### Individual Items

For each item, check the main box to **delete the directory and all files**.
Optionally, also check the вЂњfiles onlyвЂќ sub-option to **keep the directory**
structure but remove all files inside.

- [ ] **Database directory (schema, migrations, seeds, queries)** (`db`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`db:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Database schema definitions, migration scripts, SQL seed data, and example queries for SQL-based projects.
  - **Downsides:** Lose database scaffolding (schema, migrations, seeds). Must recreate from scratch if you add a database layer later.
  - Contents:
    - `db/` *(directory + all contents)*
    - `scripts/sql/` *(directory + all contents)*
    - `src/simple_python_boilerplate/sql/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Experiments / scratch code directory** (`experiments`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`experiments:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Notebook-style scratch code area for testing ideas, prototyping APIs, and exploring data without polluting src/.
  - **Downsides:** Lose a dedicated scratch-work area. You'll need to create a new directory for ad-hoc experiments.
  - Contents:
    - `experiments/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Local data / state directory (var/)** (`var`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`var:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Local runtime data such as SQLite databases, log files, and application state. Gitignored for sensitive data.
  - **Downsides:** Lose the sample SQLite database and the convention for runtime data storage. Scripts that reference var/ will break.
  - Contents:
    - `var/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Security-policy & issue templates (docs/templates/)** (`docs-templates`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`docs-templates:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Pre-built security policy template and GitHub issue/PR templates for standardized contributor experience.
  - **Downsides:** Lose pre-written templates. You'll need to write issue and PR templates from scratch if you want them later.
  - Contents:
    - `docs/templates/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Container files (Containerfile, docker-compose, build & scan workflows)** (`container`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`container:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Multi-stage Containerfile, docker-compose for local development, container structure tests, and CI workflows for building and scanning container images.
  - **Downsides:** Lose container deployment setup including multi-stage builds, security scanning, and structure tests. Significant effort to recreate.
  - Contents:
    - `Containerfile` *(file)*
    - `docker-compose.yml` *(file)*
    - `container-structure-test.yml` *(file)*
    - `.github/workflows/container-build.yml` *(file)*
    - `.github/workflows/container-scan.yml` *(file)*
    - `scripts/_container_common.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/test_containerfile.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/test_containerfile.sh` *(file)*
    - `scripts/test_docker_compose.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/test_docker_compose.sh` *(file)*

- [ ] **Optional workflow templates (.github/workflows-optional/)** (`optional-workflows`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`optional-workflows:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Extra GitHub Actions workflow templates that can be moved into .github/workflows/ when needed (e.g. nightly builds, performance benchmarks).
  - **Downsides:** Lose pre-built optional workflow templates. Can be recreated from GitHub Actions documentation if needed.
  - Contents:
    - `.github/workflows-optional/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Label management files (labels/, apply scripts)** (`labels`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`labels:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Standardized GitHub label definitions (baseline + extended sets) and scripts to apply them via the GitHub API.
  - **Downsides:** Lose automated label management. Must configure GitHub labels manually through the web UI.
  - Contents:
    - `labels/` *(directory + all contents)*
    - `scripts/apply_labels.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/apply-labels.sh` *(file)*

- [ ] **Repo doctor checks (repo_doctor.d/, doctor scripts)** (`repo-doctor`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`repo-doctor:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Repository health check system with TOML rule profiles for CI, Python, docs, containers, database, and security. Includes environment and git diagnostics.
  - **Downsides:** Lose automated repository health monitoring and diagnostics. Manual inspection required to catch missing files, config drift, and structural issues.
  - Contents:
    - `repo_doctor.d/` *(directory + all contents)*
    - `scripts/repo_doctor.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/doctor.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/env_doctor.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/git_doctor.py` *(file)*
    - `scripts/_doctor_common.py` *(file)*

- [ ] **MkDocs hooks (mkdocs-hooks/)** (`mkdocs-hooks`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`mkdocs-hooks:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Build-time MkDocs hooks that auto-generate command references, resolve repo links, and include template content during documentation builds.
  - **Downsides:** MkDocs builds will fail if hooks are referenced in mkdocs.yml. Remove hook entries from mkdocs.yml before or after stripping.
  - Contents:
    - `mkdocs-hooks/` *(directory + all contents)*

- [ ] **Repo sauron report & script** (`repo-sauron`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`repo-sauron:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Repository statistics dashboard that generates a comprehensive Markdown report with file counts, language breakdown, git history, test coverage, and directory tree.
  - **Downsides:** Lose repo analytics dashboard. No built-in replacement for at-a-glance repository statistics.
  - Contents:
    - `scripts/repo_sauron.py` *(file)*
    - `repo-sauron-report.md` *(file)*

- [ ] **Git configuration reference** (`git-config-ref`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`git-config-ref:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Reference document covering recommended Git configuration settings, aliases, and workflow tips.
  - **Downsides:** Lose reference material. Consult official Git docs or create your own reference instead.
  - Contents:
    - `git-config-reference.md` *(file)*

- [ ] **Test configuration reference** (`test-config-ref`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`test-config-ref:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Reference document for pytest configuration, fixtures, markers, and testing conventions used in this project.
  - **Downsides:** Lose test setup reference. Consult pytest docs or pyproject.toml [tool.pytest] for configuration.
  - Contents:
    - `test-config-ref.md` *(file)*

- [ ] **Commit report** (`commit-report`)
  - [ ] Files only вЂ” keep empty directory structure (`commit-report:files-only`)
  - **Description:** Generated report summarizing recent commit history, conventional commit breakdown, and authorship statistics.
  - **Downsides:** Lose commit tracking document. Use git log directly or regenerate with a custom script.
  - Contents:
    - `commit-report.md` *(file)*

---

## Template Cleanup

These items exist to support the template repository itself.
Check the boxes for items you want to clean up.

> **Note:** Each item has trade-offs. See the
> [Disclaimers & Downsides](#disclaimers--downsides) section
> at the bottom for full details on each item.
> You can always recover files from git history.

- [ ] **ADR files (docs/adr/ вЂ” template design decisions)** (`adr-files`)
- [ ] **Developer notes (docs/notes/ вЂ” learning, resources, TODOs, etc.)** (`docs-notes`)
- [ ] **Design docs (docs/design/ вЂ” architecture, tool decisions, CI/CD design, database, etc.)** (`docs-design`)
- [ ] **Reference docs (docs/reference/ вЂ” API, commands, inventory, etc.)** (`docs-reference`)
- [ ] **Development guides (docs/development/ вЂ” dev setup, PR guide, command workflows, etc.)** (`docs-development`)
- [ ] **User guides (docs/guide/ вЂ” getting started, troubleshooting, etc.)** (`docs-guide`)
- [ ] **Placeholder source & test files (clear src/ and tests/ content)** (`placeholder-code`)
- [ ] **Template utility scripts (scripts/ вЂ” most helper scripts)** (`utility-scripts`)
- [ ] **Advanced workflows (keep only essential CI subset)** (`advanced-workflows`)

## Disclaimers & Downsides

Detailed trade-offs for each strippable and cleanup item.
Read these before making your selections above.

### Optional Directories

#### `db` вЂ” Database directory (schema, migrations, seeds, queries)

**What it is:** Database schema definitions, migration scripts, SQL seed data, and example queries for SQL-based projects.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose database scaffolding (schema, migrations, seeds). Must recreate from scratch if you add a database layer later.

**Files affected:**
- `db/` вњ…
- `scripts/sql/` вњ…
- `src/simple_python_boilerplate/sql/` вњ…

#### `experiments` вЂ” Experiments / scratch code directory

**What it is:** Notebook-style scratch code area for testing ideas, prototyping APIs, and exploring data without polluting src/.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose a dedicated scratch-work area. You'll need to create a new directory for ad-hoc experiments.

**Files affected:**
- `experiments/` вњ…

#### `var` вЂ” Local data / state directory (var/)

**What it is:** Local runtime data such as SQLite databases, log files, and application state. Gitignored for sensitive data.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose the sample SQLite database and the convention for runtime data storage. Scripts that reference var/ will break.

**Files affected:**
- `var/` вњ…

#### `docs-templates` вЂ” Security-policy & issue templates (docs/templates/)

**What it is:** Pre-built security policy template and GitHub issue/PR templates for standardized contributor experience.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose pre-written templates. You'll need to write issue and PR templates from scratch if you want them later.

**Files affected:**
- `docs/templates/` вњ…

#### `container` вЂ” Container files (Containerfile, docker-compose, build & scan workflows)

**What it is:** Multi-stage Containerfile, docker-compose for local development, container structure tests, and CI workflows for building and scanning container images.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose container deployment setup including multi-stage builds, security scanning, and structure tests. Significant effort to recreate.

**Files affected:**
- `Containerfile` вњ…
- `docker-compose.yml` вњ…
- `container-structure-test.yml` вњ…
- `.github/workflows/container-build.yml` вњ…
- `.github/workflows/container-scan.yml` вњ…
- `scripts/_container_common.py` вњ…
- `scripts/test_containerfile.py` вњ…
- `scripts/test_containerfile.sh` вњ…
- `scripts/test_docker_compose.py` вњ…
- `scripts/test_docker_compose.sh` вњ…

#### `optional-workflows` вЂ” Optional workflow templates (.github/workflows-optional/)

**What it is:** Extra GitHub Actions workflow templates that can be moved into .github/workflows/ when needed (e.g. nightly builds, performance benchmarks).

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose pre-built optional workflow templates. Can be recreated from GitHub Actions documentation if needed.

**Files affected:**
- `.github/workflows-optional/` вњ…

#### `labels` вЂ” Label management files (labels/, apply scripts)

**What it is:** Standardized GitHub label definitions (baseline + extended sets) and scripts to apply them via the GitHub API.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose automated label management. Must configure GitHub labels manually through the web UI.

**Files affected:**
- `labels/` вњ…
- `scripts/apply_labels.py` вњ…
- `scripts/apply-labels.sh` вњ…

#### `repo-doctor` вЂ” Repo doctor checks (repo_doctor.d/, doctor scripts)

**What it is:** Repository health check system with TOML rule profiles for CI, Python, docs, containers, database, and security. Includes environment and git diagnostics.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose automated repository health monitoring and diagnostics. Manual inspection required to catch missing files, config drift, and structural issues.

**Files affected:**
- `repo_doctor.d/` вњ…
- `scripts/repo_doctor.py` вњ…
- `scripts/doctor.py` вњ…
- `scripts/env_doctor.py` вњ…
- `scripts/git_doctor.py` вњ…
- `scripts/_doctor_common.py` вњ…

#### `mkdocs-hooks` вЂ” MkDocs hooks (mkdocs-hooks/)

**What it is:** Build-time MkDocs hooks that auto-generate command references, resolve repo links, and include template content during documentation builds.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** MkDocs builds will fail if hooks are referenced in mkdocs.yml. Remove hook entries from mkdocs.yml before or after stripping.

**Files affected:**
- `mkdocs-hooks/` вњ…

#### `repo-sauron` вЂ” Repo sauron report & script

**What it is:** Repository statistics dashboard that generates a comprehensive Markdown report with file counts, language breakdown, git history, test coverage, and directory tree.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose repo analytics dashboard. No built-in replacement for at-a-glance repository statistics.

**Files affected:**
- `scripts/repo_sauron.py` вњ…
- `repo-sauron-report.md` вњ…

#### `git-config-ref` вЂ” Git configuration reference

**What it is:** Reference document covering recommended Git configuration settings, aliases, and workflow tips.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose reference material. Consult official Git docs or create your own reference instead.

**Files affected:**
- `git-config-reference.md` вњ…

#### `test-config-ref` вЂ” Test configuration reference

**What it is:** Reference document for pytest configuration, fixtures, markers, and testing conventions used in this project.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose test setup reference. Consult pytest docs or pyproject.toml [tool.pytest] for configuration.

**Files affected:**
- `test-config-ref.md` вњ…

#### `commit-report` вЂ” Commit report

**What it is:** Generated report summarizing recent commit history, conventional commit breakdown, and authorship statistics.

вљ пёЏ **Downsides of deletion:** Lose commit tracking document. Use git log directly or regenerate with a custom script.

**Files affected:**
- `commit-report.md` вњ…

### Template Cleanup Items

#### `adr-files` вЂ” ADR files (docs/adr/ вЂ” template design decisions)

> вљ  Removes all numbered ADR files and subdirectories (e.g. archive/) from docs/adr/. Keeps README.md and template.md. These ADRs document decisions made for the TEMPLATE repository вЂ” why Ruff over Black, why src/ layout, why Hatch, etc. They do NOT describe decisions in YOUR project. Removing them deletes the rationale behind the tooling and patterns you inherited, which can make it harder for future contributors to understand why things are set up this way. Consider keeping them as reference until you've written your own ADRs.

#### `docs-notes` вЂ” Developer notes (docs/notes/ вЂ” learning, resources, TODOs, etc.)

> вљ  Removes the entire docs/notes/ directory, including: learning.md (Python packaging lessons, mental models for Hatch vs Hatchling, lockfile strategies), resources.md (curated links to official docs for every tool in the stack), tool-comparison.md (side-by-side evaluations of formatters, linters, type checkers, task runners, etc.), todo.md (roadmap and checklist of remaining template work), archive.md (completed items for historical context), and README.md. No project functionality depends on these files, but they contain valuable context вЂ” especially learning.md and resources.md вЂ” that can accelerate onboarding for developers unfamiliar with the tooling stack. Consider keeping them as reference material, or extracting useful content into your own docs before removing.

#### `docs-design` вЂ” Design docs (docs/design/ вЂ” architecture, tool decisions, CI/CD design, database, etc.)

> вљ  Removes the entire docs/design/ directory, including: architecture.md, ci-cd-design.md, database.md, tool-decisions.md, and any future files added there. These document the 'why' behind CI/CD pipeline design, dependency choices, database schema rationale, and overall system architecture. The copilot-instructions.md file references these docs вЂ” Copilot will lose context for architectural questions. Contributors will have no written rationale for the project's structural decisions.

#### `docs-reference` вЂ” Reference docs (docs/reference/ вЂ” API, commands, inventory, etc.)

> вљ  Removes the entire docs/reference/ directory, including: api.md, commands.md, index.md, template-inventory.md, and README.md. The command reference is auto-generated by generate_command_reference.py and will need to be regenerated if you add new scripts. The template inventory is only useful during initial setup. The mkdocs-hooks/generate_commands.py hook will fail if docs/reference/ is missing вЂ” update mkdocs.yml nav and remove the hook if you strip this.

#### `docs-development` вЂ” Development guides (docs/development/ вЂ” dev setup, PR guide, command workflows, etc.)

> вљ  Removes the entire docs/development/ directory, including: dev-setup.md, pull-requests.md, developer-commands.md, command-workflows.md, development.md, and README.md. These are developer onboarding docs covering environment setup, PR workflow, and available commands. Removing these makes it harder for new contributors to get started. mkdocs.yml nav links to these pages вЂ” update it to avoid broken links. Consider replacing with your own project-specific guides.

#### `docs-guide` вЂ” User guides (docs/guide/ вЂ” getting started, troubleshooting, etc.)

> вљ  Removes the entire docs/guide/ directory, including: getting-started.md, troubleshooting.md, and README.md. These provide end-user onboarding and common issue resolution. mkdocs.yml nav references these pages вЂ” update it to avoid broken links. Consider replacing with project-specific guides rather than deleting outright.

#### `placeholder-code` вЂ” Placeholder source & test files (clear src/ and tests/ content)

> вљ  Replaces placeholder code in src/ modules (api.py, cli.py, engine.py, main.py) and test files (test_example.py) with minimal stubs. Also removes non-__init__.py files from the dev_tools/ subdirectory if present. Keeps the file structure so you can fill in your own code. __init__.py, _version.py, conftest.py, and py.typed are preserved as-is. Entry points in pyproject.toml still reference these files вЂ” update them if you rename or restructure.

#### `utility-scripts` вЂ” Template utility scripts (scripts/ вЂ” most helper scripts)

> вљ  Removes 7 scripts and the precommit/ directory: archive_todos.py, changelog_check.py, check_known_issues.py, check_todos.py, dep_versions.py, generate_command_reference.py, workflow_versions.py, and scripts/precommit/. bootstrap.py, clean.py, and customize.py are kept. Removing these also means Taskfile tasks and pre-commit hooks that call them will break вЂ” update Taskfile.yml and .pre-commit-config.yaml accordingly. Some GitHub Actions workflows may also reference these scripts.

#### `advanced-workflows` вЂ” Advanced workflows (keep only essential CI subset)

> вљ  Deletes ALL .yml files in .github/workflows/ EXCEPT the essential set: ci-gate, test, lint-format, type-check, coverage, security-audit, dependency-review, docs-build. This removes ~28 workflows including: release automation (release-please), container builds & scans, nightly security scans, Scorecard, SBOM generation, spellcheck, label management, welcome bot, stale issues, and more. You will lose significant CI/CD automation вЂ” including automated releases and container publishing. Non-.yml files (e.g. .instructions.md) are kept. You can re-add individual workflows from git history or the .github/workflows-optional/ directory.

---

## Quick Reference

| Command | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- |
| `python scripts/customize.py` | Generate this config file |
| `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run` | Preview changes |
| `python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md` | Apply changes |
| `python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive --project-name NAME ...` | Direct CLI mode |
| `python scripts/customize.py --enable-workflows owner/repo` | Enable workflows only |
| `python scripts/customize.py --export-config custom-path.md` | Export to custom path |

---

## Applying Changes from This File

Edit the values and checkboxes above, then apply:

```bash
# Preview changes first
python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md --dry-run

# Apply changes
python scripts/customize.py --apply-from customize-config.md
```

**How it works:**

- Values in the Project Identity table replace the template placeholders.
- Checked boxes (`[x]`) activate that option (strip, cleanup, private repo).
- Unchecked boxes (`[ ]`) leave that option inactive.
- **Bulk actions** override individual selections when checked.
- **Files only** options delete files but preserve directory structures.
- Use `--dry-run` to preview all changes before applying.
- Use `--force` to skip the already-customized safety check.
